title: Delamotte & Salon Champagne Masterclass: DFWE New York 2026
summary: Master Sommelier Pascaline Lepeltier reports on the sold-out Champagne masterclass she hosted with Delamotte and Salon’s export director Cristian Rimoldi.
content: <div class="author-description"><p>Growing up in the Loire, Pascaline Lepeltier turned from an academic path in philosophy to wine. In 2018, she won the Union de la Sommellerie Française Best Sommelier of France title, and became the first woman ever to win the prestigious Meilleur Ouvrier de France award in Sommellerie. She has been based in New York since 2009 where she runs the beverage program of Chambers restaurant, and her first solo book A Thousand Vines will be published in English next year in the UK and the US by Mitchell Beazley.</p></div>

title: Spirits briefing: Whisky in 'orange wine' casks and how to make a Colomba cocktail
summary: For the garden bar this summer...
content: <div class="author-description"><p class="p1"><span class="s1">Julie Sheppard joined the Decanter team in 2018 and is Regional Editor for Australia, New Zealand and South Africa &amp; Spirits Editor. </span></p><p class="p1"><span class="s1">Before Decanter, she worked for a range of drinks and food titles, including as managing editor of both <em>Imbibe</em> and <em>Square Meal</em>, associate publisher of </span><em>The Drinks Business</em><span class="s1">, senior editor of the Octopus Publishing Group and Supplements editor of <em>Harpers Wine &amp; Spirit</em>. As a contributor, she has over 20 years’ experience writing <span class="Apple-converted-space">about food, drink and travel </span>for a wide range of publications, including <em><span class="Apple-converted-space">Condé Nast Traveller, </span></em></span><span class="s1"><em>Delicious</em>, <em>Waitrose Kitchen</em>, <em>Waitrose Drinks</em>, <em>Time Out</em> and national newspapers including <em>The Telegraph</em> and <em>The Sunday Times</em>.</span></p><p class="p1"><span class="s1">.</span></p></div>

title: Five reasons why Washington bubbly should be your Fourth of July fizz
summary: Patriotically pop the cork on these Washington state sparkling wines to celebrate the 250th anniversary of the United States Declaration of Independence.
content: <p>Gwendolyn Elliott is a wine writer and editor based in Walla Walla, Washington who won the 2024 Allen Shoup Memorial Wine Writing Fellowship. She pens the ‘Wine is a Food Group’ column for the <em>Walla Walla Union-Bulletin </em>and also writes on her own website while studying for the Wine & Spirits Education Trust’s Level 3 certification. Originally from New York State, she graduated from Nazareth College of Rochester with a Philosophy degree, but moved to Washington state to nurture a fledgling music writing career, working as Music Editor for <em>Seattle Weekly</em> among other titles. While working in restaurants to support her writing, she developed a love of wine and food, particularly plant-based culinary representation in wine, which led her to start ‘Beet Street’, a vegetarian dining column at<em> Seattle Weekly</em>. She has since worked with award-winning vegan chef Miyoko Schinner and Pepper Bridge Winery to produce Walla Walla’s first-ever vegan winemaker’s dinner. </p>

title: Success for English wine at DWWA 2026
summary: The breadth of English wine was on display at Decanter World Wine Awards 2026, with top winners spanning from aged sparkling wines to characterful still styles.
content: <div class="author-description"><p>Chris Mercer is a Bristol-based freelance editor and journalist who spent nearly four years as digital editor of <strong>Decanter.com</strong>, having previously been <em>Decanter’s</em> news editor across online and print.</p><p>He has written about, and reported on, the wine and food sectors for more than 10 years for both consumer and trade media.</p><p>Chris first became interested in the wine world while living in Languedoc-Roussillon after completing a journalism Masters in the UK. These days, his love of wine commonly tests his budgeting skills.</p><p>Beyond wine, Chris also has an MSc in food policy and has a particular interest in sustainability issues. He has also been a food judge at the UK’s Great Taste Awards.</p></div>

title: Five summer wine hacks and 20 bottles to pair with sunny days
summary: Wines to reach for as the mercury rises...
content: <p>Natalie is Decanter's France editor, commissioning and writing content on French wines (excluding Bordeaux) across print and digital. She writes Decanter's coverage of Languedoc wines, as well as a monthly magazine column, The Ethical Drinker, which unpicks the thorny topic of sustainability in wine. </p><p>It was during her time studying for a French and Italian degree that Natalie began her foray into wine: tutoring French in exchange for WSET lessons in her spare time (she now realises who got the better deal!). She moved to the Languedoc after graduating to work for a vineyard tour company, before returning to the UK in 2016 to join the tastings team at Decanter.</p><p>She worked across Decanter's panel tastings and wine competitions before becoming awards competition manager, overseeing the competitive and judging elements of the Decanter World Wine Awards, Decanter Asia Wine Awards and Retailer Awards, and completing her WSET Diploma in 2019.</p><p>In 2021 she made the shift to the Decanter editorial team, and is now the Regional Editor for France (outside of Bordeaux and Burgundy).</p><p>She will always be drawn to the wines of the Languedoc and Roussillon, but her wine tastes are wide-ranging and she can't resist a glass of Manzanilla Sherry or the lure of an obscure grape variety.</p>

title: Pairing wine with seafood: Expert advice and five perfect matches to try tonight
summary: From golden rules to offbeat suggestions...
content: <div class="author-description"><p class="p1"><span class="s1">Fiona Sims is a food, drink and travel writer with 25 years’ experience. Aside from Decanter, she has written for The Times, The Telegraph, The Guardian, National Geographic Food and The Caterer. As a Decanter contributor, she writes travel, bar and restaurant guides, plus interviews with high-profile wine lovers like William Boyd. She co-founded the website the The2Fionas.com with fellow writer, Fiona Beckett.</span></p></div>

title: Canada Day pairings: BC wines for 10 Canadian summer dishes
summary: A west coast feast...
content: <div class="author-description"><p>Nicole MacKay is a wine writer and editor based in Calgary, Canada. She’s passionate about everything related to wine, food, and travel and has more than a decade of wine industry experience working with brands, distributors and liquor boards. Nicole is the current managing editor for the SOMM TV Magazine and a freelance writer for a number of wine and drinks publications. She’s a Spanish Wine Scholar through the Wine Scholar Guild and holds her Level 3 certificate from the Wine &amp; Spirit Education Trust.</p></div>

title: National treasures: America's old vines and their dynamic and delicious wines
summary: A cherished but endangered resource...
content: <div class="author-description"><p>Brazilian-born Bay Area local Ana Carolina has a degree in journalism and got her start as a daily business reporter for the largest daily newspaper in Northeastern Brazil, the Diário do Nordeste. Upon moving to the San Francisco Bay Area, she worked as a journalist for the bilingual San Francisco newspaper El Tecolote. She is a certified sommelier, having worked in both wine and fine dining in San Francisco. She pursued a career in wine publishing before returning to her roots as a writer.</p></div>

title: Blended Revolution: How South American winemakers find terroir expression through more than just one grape
summary: When people, cultures and grapes meet...
content: <div class="author-description"><p><span style="font-weight: 400">Ines is Decanter’s regional editor for Spain, Portugal and South America. Born and raised in Lisbon, Portugal, she grew up chasing her grandfather among his vines in Ribatejo and thus her love for all things wine began. After completing her Masters Degree in Architecture, Ines worked as a project manager while writing about wine and doing cellar consulting on the side. After moving to London in 2015, she decided to dedicate herself fully to the wine industry and joined the sommelier team at Michelin-starred Spring, Somerset House. Stints at Noble Rot and The Laughing Heart followed, while completing her WSET Diploma in Wines and Spirits. Her work as a judge and writer eventually became her full time commitment and she joined Decanter in 2019 as wine database editor. </span></p></div>

title: Summer sipping: The fresh white wine revolution in Paso Robles
summary: A crisper taste of Paso...
content: <div class="author-description"><p>Brianne Cohen is a Los Angeles-based event producer, wine educator, and wine writer. She now offers both in-person (and virtual) wine-tasting experiences for her corporate clients while highlighting diverse (i.e. Black, BIPOC, female, and LGBT) owned wineries. Brianne regularly judges at international wine competitions, including the International Wine Challenge (IWC) in London and holds the WSET Diploma certificate. She writes on her own blog and for outlets such as Decanter, Monarch Wine, Matador, SommTV, and Edible. She also holds a Master of Business Administration from Loyola Marymount University.</p></div>
